suite.name: Ess Battc00227 Ess

test.name: BATTC00227: Verify ESS user is able to add / edit / delete temporary availability in web

kw.name: Login And Launch WFM Web App
Get Config Value
kw.doc: Retrieves a configuration value by key with optional default fallback.

kw.name: Perform Direct Login To WFM Using User Credentials
Get Config Value
kw.doc: Retrieves a configuration value by key with optional default fallback.

kw.name: Launch Browser
Connect To ZTest Cloud Browser
kw.doc: Connect to zTest cloud browser using WebSocket URL with credentials from environment variables. This keyword is used when running tests on zTest cloud platform. It retrieves CLOUD_USERNAME and CLOUD_TOKEN from environment variables to establish a connection. Example usage: | Connect To ZTest Cloud Browser

kw.name: Convert To Lower Case
kw.doc: Converts string to lower case.

kw.name: Set Variable If
kw.doc: Sets variable based on the given condition.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: New Browser
kw.doc: Create a new playwright Browser with specified options.

kw.name: Set Variable If
kw.doc: Sets variable based on the given condition.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: New Browser
kw.doc: Create a new playwright Browser with specified options.

kw.name: Set Variable If
kw.doc: Sets variable based on the given condition.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: New Browser
kw.msg: {"browser": "chromium", "headless": false, "args": ["--start-maximized", "--incognito"], "channel": "msedge", "chromiumSandbox": false, "devtools": false, "handleSIGHUP": true, "handleSIGINT": true, "handleSIGTERM": true, "slowMo": 0.0, "timeout": 30000.0, "tracesDir": "C:\\Users\\MK6843\\PycharmProjects\\sws_wfm_test_automation\\results\\results_QA27_B0\\browser\\traces\\temp\\e9cc6f1e-8054-4db8-b2cb-e9775ec906f6"}
kw.doc: Create a new playwright Browser with specified options.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: New Browser
kw.doc: Create a new playwright Browser with specified options.

kw.name: Fail
kw.arg: Unsupported browser: ${browser_name}. Supported browsers are: chrome, firefox, edge, safari
kw.doc: Fails the test with the given message and optionally alters its tags.
Launch Browser based on the browser name provided. Supported browsers: chrome, firefox, edge, safari | Usage Example : | Launch Browser chrome | Launch Browser firefox is_headless_mode=False

kw.name: Launch WFM Web Application
Set Selector Prefix
kw.doc: Sets the prefix for all selectors in the given scope.

kw.name: Open New Browser Context With Page
Set Library Search Order
kw.doc: Sets the resolution order to use when a name matches multiple keywords.

kw.name: Set To Dictionary
kw.doc: Adds the given ``key_value_pairs`` and/or ``items`` to the ``dictionary``.

kw.name: New Context
kw.msg: {'acceptDownloads': True, 'bypassCSP': False, 'forcedColors': 'none', 'ignoreHTTPSErrors': False, 'javaScriptEnabled': True, 'offline': False, 'recordVideo': {'size': {'width': 1920, 'height': 1080}, 'dir': 'C:\\LOGS\\Browser\\videos'}, 'reducedMotion': 'no-preference', 'serviceWorkers': 'allow', 'viewport': None}
kw.doc: Create a new BrowserContext with specified options.

kw.name: New Page
kw.msg: Successfully initialized new page object and opened url: https://wfmproductqa27.reflexisinc.com/kernel/views/authenticate/W/TESTING1.view
kw.doc: Open a new Page.
Open a new browser context with the given web page url. | Usage Example : | Open New Browser Context With Page https://www.google.com

kw.name: Wait Until Page Is Loaded
Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive domcontentloaded in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.

kw.name: Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive load in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Ignore Error
Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive networkidle in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.
Runs the given keyword with the given arguments and ignores possible error.
Waits until the current page is fully loaded. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default to PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT | Example usage: | Wait Until Page Is Loaded
Launches the WFM Web Application

kw.name: Update Default Language To English Of Non Mywork Login Page On Web
Get Config Value
kw.doc: Retrieves a configuration value by key with optional default fallback.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
kw.doc: Waits until the specified element is visible, stable, and ready for interaction on the webpage. This keyword ensures the element is fully loaded and not animating before proceeding. Use this for both display verification and pre-interaction element readiness checks. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element. | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default is LONG_TIMEOUT. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not ready. Optional. | Example usage: | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=main-content | | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=submit-button timeout=10 |

kw.name: Click Element On Webpage
kw.doc: Clicks the specified element on the webpage. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element to click. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not visible. Optional. | Example usage: | Click Element On Webpage id=submit-btn

kw.name: Click Element On Webpage
kw.doc: Clicks the specified element on the webpage. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element to click. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not visible. Optional. | Example usage: | Click Element On Webpage id=submit-btn

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
kw.doc: Waits until the specified element is visible, stable, and ready for interaction on the webpage. This keyword ensures the element is fully loaded and not animating before proceeding. Use this for both display verification and pre-interaction element readiness checks. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element. | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default is LONG_TIMEOUT. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not ready. Optional. | Example usage: | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=main-content | | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=submit-button timeout=10 |
This keyword will update the default language to English on login page if enabled for the environment.

kw.name: Switch User Locale Before Login
kw.doc: This keyword will switch user locale to any locale on login page

kw.name: Fill And Submit WFM Login Form
Get Dynamic New Hire User
kw.doc: Retrieves the dynamically generated new hire user from pabot-shared cache for parallel execution safety. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | user_key | Key to retrieve from dynamic employee cache (e.g., ESSNH1_STORE2). | Example usage: | ${essnh1}= | Get Dynamic New Hire User | ESSNH1_STORE2 | | Log | ${essnh1}[username] | | Add New Blank Template ... | ${essnh1}[displayName] |

kw.name: Enter Username
kw.doc: Fills in the username field on the login page. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | username | Username to enter in the login form. | Example usage: | Enter Username testuser

kw.name: Enter Password
kw.doc: Fills in the password field on the login page. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | password | Password to enter in the login form. | Example usage: | Enter Password mypassword

kw.name: Enter Username
Wait Until Element Is Present On Webpage
Wait For Elements State
kw.msg: Waited for Element with selector //input[@type='text' and @spellcheck='false'] at state attached
kw.doc: Waits for the element found by ``selector`` to satisfy state option.

Waits until the specified element is present in the DOM and attached to the webpage. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element. | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not present. Optional. | Example usage: | Wait Until Element Is Present On Webpage id=footer

kw.name: Type Text
kw.msg: Types the text 'ZEMP000002' in the given field.
kw.doc: Types the given ``txt`` into the text field found by ``selector``.
Fills in the username field on the login page. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | username | Username to enter in the login form. | Example usage: | Enter Username testuser

kw.name: Enter Password
Type Secret
kw.doc: Types the given secret from ``variable_name`` into the text field found by ``selector``.
Fills in the password field on the login page. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | password | Password to enter in the login form. | Example usage: | Enter Password mypassword

kw.name: Click Login Button
Wait For Elements State
kw.msg: Waited for Element with selector button.login-button at state visible
kw.doc: Waits for the element found by ``selector`` to satisfy state option.

kw.name: Click
kw.doc: Simulates mouse click on the element found by ``selector``.
Clicks the Login button on the login page.

kw.name: Wait Until Page Is Loaded
Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive domcontentloaded in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.

kw.name: Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive load in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Ignore Error
Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive networkidle in 30000.0

TimeoutError: page.waitForLoadState: Timeout 30000ms exceeded. =========================== logs =========================== "commit" event fired "domcontentloaded" event fired "load" event fired ============================================================
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.
kw.status: TimeoutError: page.waitForLoadState: Timeout 30000ms exceeded. =========================== logs =========================== "commit" event fired "domcontentloaded" event fired "load" event fired ============================================================

kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments and ignores possible error.
Waits until the current page is fully loaded. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default to PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT | Example usage: | Wait Until Page Is Loaded

Logs in to the WFM application using the given user key. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | user_key | For dynamic users: key to lookup in SUITE_DYNAMIC_EMPLOYEES (e.g. ESSNH1_STORE2). For regular users: key to lookup in ALL_CREDENTIALS (e.g. SM1_STORE1). | | is_dynamic_user | Optional flag to indicate if the test is for a dynamic user. Defaults to False. | Example usage: | Fill And Submit WFM Login Form ESS5_STORE1 | Fill And Submit WFM Login Form SM1_STORE1 | Fill And Submit WFM Login Form ESSNH1_STORE2 is_dynamic_user=True
Logs into the WFM application using provided user credentials. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | user_key | Key for user credentials (e.g. SM1_STORE1, ESS1_STORE1, SYSADMIN). |

kw.name: Perform Login Using Sudo Flow
kw.doc: Orchestrates sudo login flow with multiple authentication strategies. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | user_key | Key for user credentials. |

kw.name: Verify Successful Login To WFM Application
Get Config Value
kw.doc: Retrieves a configuration value by key with optional default fallback.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
kw.arg: error_message=User Profile Icon Not Seen After Login. Login might have failed.
kw.doc: Waits until the specified element is visible, stable, and ready for interaction on the webpage. This keyword ensures the element is fully loaded and not animating before proceeding. Use this for both display verification and pre-interaction element readiness checks. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element. | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default is LONG_TIMEOUT. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not ready. Optional. | Example usage: | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=main-content | | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=submit-button timeout=10 |

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
Wait For Elements State
kw.msg: Waited for Element with selector #userSettings at state visible
kw.doc: Waits for the element found by ``selector`` to satisfy state option.

kw.name: Wait For Elements State
kw.msg: Waited for Element with selector #userSettings at state stable
kw.doc: Waits for the element found by ``selector`` to satisfy state option.

kw.arg: error_message=User Profile Icon Not Seen After Login. Login might have failed.
kw.doc: Waits until the specified element is visible, stable, and ready for interaction on the webpage. This keyword ensures the element is fully loaded and not animating before proceeding. Use this for both display verification and pre-interaction element readiness checks. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element. | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default is LONG_TIMEOUT. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not ready. Optional. | Example usage: | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=main-content | | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=submit-button timeout=10 |

kw.name: Wait Until Page Is Loaded
Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive domcontentloaded in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.

kw.name: Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive load in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Ignore Error
Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive networkidle in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.
Runs the given keyword with the given arguments and ignores possible error.
Waits until the current page is fully loaded. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default to PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT | Example usage: | Wait Until Page Is Loaded
Verifies successful login to WFM application by checking for user profile icon.

Logs in and launches the WFM web application for any user (direct or sudo flow). | =Flow= | =Description= | | DIRECT_LOGIN True | Launch app_url and perform direct login with username/password. | | DIRECT_LOGIN False | Reuse existing sudo session URL or establish new sudo session via kernel. | | STRADMN profile | After sudo session, switch to target store profile (STORE_ADMIN). | | Session reuse | If LOGIN_SESSION_URL for suite already populated it is reused (faster). | | =Arguments= | =Description= | | user_key | Key for user credentials (e.g. SM1_STORE1, ESS1_STORE1, SYSADMIN). | | is_dynamic_user | Optional flag to indicate if the test is for a dynamic user. Defaults to False. | | locale | Locale to switch to on login page. e.g es_MX, en_US, etc| Example usage: | Login And Launch WFM Web App user_key=SM1_STORE1 | Login And Launch WFM Web App user_key=ESS1_STORE1 | Login And Launch WFM Web App user_key=SYSADMIN | Login And Launch WFM Web App user_key=SM1_STORE1 locale=es_MX

kw.name: Navigate To ESS My Availability Page
Navigate To Specific Page On Web
Get Locators For Page Navigation
kw.doc: Returns locators for page navigation based on provided parameters. Handles module-only, module+parent, and module+parent+child navigation.

kw.doc: Sets the prefix for all selectors in the given scope.

kw.name: Navigate Via Mywork Menu
kw.doc: Navigates to the menu as per mentioned in the arguments for MyWork enabled environments. Supports module-only navigation when parent_submenu_id is NA.

kw.name: Wait Until Page Is Loaded
kw.doc: Waits until the current page is fully loaded. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default to PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT | Example usage: | Wait Until Page Is Loaded

kw.name: Create Combined String
kw.doc: Short description.

kw.name: Create Combined String
kw.doc: Short description.

kw.name: Handle Iframe If Available
kw.doc: Check if the iframe is available, then perform operations; otherwise, skip.

kw.name: Navigate Via Non Mywork Menu
Is Element Visible On Webpage
Get Element States
kw.doc: Get the active states from the element found by ``selector``.
Checks if the specified element is visible on the screen. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | selector | Locator for the element. | Example usage: | Is Element Visible On Webpage id=main-content

kw.name: Run Keyword And Ignore Error
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments and ignores possible error.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.arg: Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
kw.doc: Runs the specified keyword and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Capture Screenshot On Webpage
kw.doc: Captures a screenshot of the current webpage and embeds it in the log.html. Example usage: | Capture Screenshot On Webpage

kw.name: Fail
kw.doc: Fails the test with the given message and optionally alters its tags.

kw.name: Check And Navigate To Main Menu On Non Mywork Menu
Is Element Visible On Webpage
kw.doc: Checks if the specified element is visible on the screen. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | selector | Locator for the element. | Example usage: | Is Element Visible On Webpage id=main-content

kw.name: Click Element On Webpage
kw.doc: Clicks the specified element on the webpage. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element to click. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not visible. Optional. | Example usage: | Click Element On Webpage id=submit-btn

kw.doc: Verifies if the element corresponding to provided locator is attached to DOM.

kw.doc: Skips the rest of the current test.

Waits until the specified element is visible, stable, and ready for interaction on the webpage. This keyword ensures the element is fully loaded and not animating before proceeding. kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive load in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Ignore Error
Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive networkidle in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.
Runs the given keyword with the given arguments and ignores possible error.
Waits until the current page is fully loaded. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default to PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT | Example usage: | Wait Until Page Is Loaded
Navigates to the menu on Non Mywork Integrated Environments
Navigates to the menu as per mentioned in the arguments. Supports module-only, module+parent, and module+parent+child navigation.

kw.name: Wait Until Page Is Loaded
Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive domcontentloaded in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.

kw.name: Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive load in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Ignore Error
Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive networkidle in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.
Runs the given keyword with the given arguments and ignores possible error.
Waits until the current page is fully loaded. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default to PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT | Example usage: | Wait Until Page Is Loaded

Navigates to a specific page on the web application based on provided parameters. Supports flexible navigation: - Module only: Navigate To Specific Page On Web rws - Module + Parent: Navigate To Specific Page On Web rws reports - Module + Parent + Child: Navigate To Specific Page On Web rws reports view_reports
Navigate To ESS -> My Availability Page

kw.name: Append To List
kw.doc: Adds ``values`` to the end of ``list``.

kw.name: Cleanup Existing Availability Requests On My Availability Page On Web
Set Selector Prefix
kw.doc: Sets the prefix for all selectors in the given scope.

kw.name: Get Config Value
kw.doc: Retrieves a configuration value by key with optional default fallback.

kw.name: Calculate Date From Week Day Offset
kw.msg: Using base week start date for calculation: 2026-06-21
kw.doc: Calculates a specific date from a week-day offset format.

kw.name: Update Locator With Dynamic Values
Replace String
kw.doc: Replaces ``search_for`` in the given ``string`` with ``replace_with``.

kw.name: Set To Dictionary
kw.doc: Adds the given ``key_value_pairs`` and/or ``items`` to the ``dictionary``.

kw.name: Format String
kw.doc: Formats a ``template`` using the given ``positional`` and ``named`` arguments.

kw.name: Replace String
kw.doc: Replaces ``search_for`` in the given ``string`` with ``replace_with``.
Generic keyword to update locator with any number of placeholder values. Uses Format String for safe parameter substitution. Handles non-breaking spaces and preserves formatting.

kw.name: Is Element Visible On Webpage
Get Element States
kw.doc: Get the active states from the element found by ``selector``.
Checks if the specified element is visible on the screen. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | selector | Locator for the element. | Example usage: | Is Element Visible On Webpage id=main-content

kw.name: Set Selector Prefix
kw.doc: Sets the prefix for all selectors in the given scope.

kw.name: Delete Availability Request On My Availability Page On Web
kw.doc: Deletes an availability request on the ESS My Availability page as ESS user. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | effective_date_offset | The week start date for the availability request in offset date format (e.g. 1_1). | | week_start_applicable | Boolean flag indicating if the week start date field is applicable for the availability request. |

kw.doc: Adds ``values`` to the end of ``list``.

kw.doc: Retrieves a configuration value by key with optional default fallback.

kw.doc: Calculates the start date of a week from a week-day offset format.

kw.name: Format String
kw.doc: Formats a ``template`` using the given ``positional`` and ``named`` arguments.

kw.name: Replace String
kw.doc: Replaces ``search_for`` in the given ``string`` with ``replace_with``.
Generic keyword to update locator with any number of placeholder values. Uses Format String for safe parameter substitution. Handles non-breaking spaces and preserves formatting.

Fill My Availability Request Fields On My Availability Page On Web
Get Config Value
kw.doc: Retrieves a configuration value by key with optional default fallback.

kw.name: Get Config Value
kw.doc: Retrieves a configuration value by key with optional default fallback.

kw.name: Click Add Button On My Availability Page On Web
Click Element On Webpage
Wait For Elements State
kw.msg: Waited for Element with selector #buttonAdd at state visible
kw.doc: Waits for the element found by ``selector`` to satisfy state option.

kw.name: Wait For Elements State
kw.msg: Waited for Element with selector #buttonAdd at state stable
kw.doc: Waits for the element found by ``selector`` to satisfy state option.

kw.name: Click
kw.doc: Simulates mouse click on the element found by ``selector``.

kw.arg: error_message=Add Request button not found or not clickable on My Availability page.
kw.doc: Clicks the specified element on the webpage. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element to click. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not visible. Optional. | Example usage: | Click Element On Webpage id=submit-btn
Clicks on the "Add Request" button on the ESS My Availability page.

kw.name: Wait For Load State
kw.msg: Waiting page load to state to receive domcontentloaded in 30000.0
kw.doc: Waits that the page reaches the required load state.

kw.name: Wait Until Loading Icon Disappears On My Availability Page On Web
Run Keyword And Return Status
Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
Wait For Elements State
kw.msg: Keyword 'Take Screenshot EMBED' could not be run on failure: Playwright process has been terminated with code 130
kw.doc: Waits for the element found by ``selector`` to satisfy state option.

kw.name: Wait For Elements State
kw.doc: Waits for the element found by ``selector`` to satisfy state option.

Waits until the specified element is visible, stable, and ready for interaction on the webpage. This keyword ensures the element is fully loaded and not animating before proceeding. Use this for both display verification and pre-interaction element readiness checks. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element. | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default is LONG_TIMEOUT. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not ready. Optional. | Example usage: | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=main-content | | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=submit-button timeout=10 |

kw.arg: Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
kw.doc: Runs the specified keyword and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Disappears On Webpage
kw.doc: Waits until the specified element disappears from the webpage. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element. | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. | Example usage: | Wait Until Element Disappears On Webpage id=loading
Waits until the page loading indicator disappears on the ESS My Availability page.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
Wait For Elements State
kw.doc: Waits for the element found by ``selector`` to satisfy state option.

kw.name: Wait For Elements State
kw.doc: Waits for the element found by ``selector`` to satisfy state option.

kw.arg: error_message=Preference dropdown not found on My Availability page after clicking Add Request button.
kw.doc: Waits until the specified element is visible, stable, and ready for interaction on the webpage. This keyword ensures the element is fully loaded and not animating before proceeding. Use this for both display verification and pre-interaction element readiness checks. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element. | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default is LONG_TIMEOUT. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not ready. Optional. | Example usage: | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=main-content | | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=submit-button timeout=10 |

kw.name: Select List Item On Webpage
kw.doc: Select an option in drop-down by index/value/text on webpage | =Arguments= | =Description= | | selector | Locator for the element. | | attribute | Attribute to select by (index/value/label). | | value | Value to select. | Example usage: | Select List Item On Webpage id=dropdown value option1

kw.name: Wait Until Loading Icon Disappears On My Availability Page On Web
kw.doc: Waits until the page loading indicator disappears on the ESS My Availability page.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.arg: Wait Until Element Disappears On Webpage
kw.doc: Runs the specified keyword and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.arg: Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
kw.doc: Runs the specified keyword and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Select Date From Date Picker On My Availability Page On Web
kw.doc: Selects the start date for availability on the ESS My Availability page using the date picker. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | field_locator | The locator for the week start date input field which triggers the date picker when clicked. | | week_date_offset | The week start date for the availability request in offset date format (e.g. 1_1). |

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Wait Until Loading Icon Disappears On My Availability Page On Web
kw.doc: Waits until the page loading indicator disappears on the ESS My Availability page.

kw.name: Add Time To Date
kw.doc: Adds time to date and returns the resulting date.

kw.name: Get Text
kw.arg: message=End date input field not found or not visible after selecting start date on My Availability page.
kw.doc: Returns text attribute of the element found by ``selector``.

kw.name: Should Be Equal
kw.arg: msg=End date ${end_date_selected} does not match expected ${end_date_calculated} based on selected start date ${start_date_selected}.
kw.doc: Fails if the given objects are unequal.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
kw.arg: error_message=Year dropdown not found on My Availability page for week-based availability requests.
kw.doc: Waits until the specified element is visible, stable, and ready for interaction on the webpage. This keyword ensures the element is fully loaded and not animating before proceeding. Use this for both display verification and pre-interaction element readiness checks. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element. | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default is LONG_TIMEOUT. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not ready. Optional. | Example usage: | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=main-content | | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=submit-button timeout=10 |

kw.name: Calculate Date From Week Day Offset
kw.doc: Calculates a specific date from a week-day offset format.

kw.name: Select List Item On Webpage
kw.doc: Select an option in drop-down by index/value/text on webpage | =Arguments= | =Description= | | selector | Locator for the element. | | attribute | Attribute to select by (index/value/label). | | value | Value to select. | Example usage: | Select List Item On Webpage id=dropdown value option1

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage
kw.arg: error_message=Week dropdown not found on My Availability page for week-based availability requests.
kw.doc: Waits until the specified element is visible, stable, and ready for interaction on the webpage. This keyword ensures the element is fully loaded and not animating before proceeding. Use this for both display verification and pre-interaction element readiness checks. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element. | | timeout | Maximum time to wait. Optional. Default is LONG_TIMEOUT. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not ready. Optional. | Example usage: | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=main-content | | Wait Until Element Is Visible On Webpage id=submit-button timeout=10 |

kw.name: Calculate Date From Week Day Offset
kw.doc: Calculates a specific date from a week-day offset format.

kw.name: Select List Item On Webpage
kw.doc: Select an option in drop-down by index/value/text on webpage | =Arguments= | =Description= | | selector | Locator for the element. | | attribute | Attribute to select by (index/value/label). | | value | Value to select. | Example usage: | Select List Item On Webpage id=dropdown value option1

kw.name: Is Element Visible On Webpage
kw.doc: Checks if the specified element is visible on the screen. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | selector | Locator for the element. | Example usage: | Is Element Visible On Webpage id=main-content

kw.name: Select List Item On Webpage
kw.doc: Select an option in drop-down by index/value/text on webpage | =Arguments= | =Description= | | selector | Locator for the element. | | attribute | Attribute to select by (index/value/label). | | value | Value to select. | Example usage: | Select List Item On Webpage id=dropdown value option1

kw.name: Click Element On Webpage
kw.arg: error_message=Totally Available button not found or not clickable on My Availability page after filling availability request fields.
kw.doc: Clicks the specified element on the webpage. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | locator | Locator for the element to click. | | error_message | Custom error message if element is not visible. Optional. | Example usage: | Click Element On Webpage id=submit-btn

Fill dropdown and date fields of ESS My Availability Request. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | week_date_offset | The week start date for the availability request in offset date format (e.g. 1_1). | | preference | The availability preference to select (e.g., "Permanent"). | | reason | The reason for the availability request. |

kw.name: Fill Availability Request Weekly Data From Template On My Availability Page On Web
kw.doc: Fills in availability request weekly data from template on the ESS My Availability page. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | availability_request_data | Key to retrieve availability request data from template file. |

kw.name: Submit Availability Request On My Availability Page On Web
kw.doc: Submits the availability request on the ESS My Availability page.

kw.name: Check And Click If Previous Button Is Visible On My Availability Page On Web
kw.doc: Checks if the Previous button is visible on the ESS My Availability page and clicks it if present.

Add ESS My Availability Request on My Availability page. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | week_date_offset | The week start date for the availability request in offset date format (e.g. 1_1). | | preference | The availability preference to select (e.g., "Permanent"). | | reason | The reason for the availability request. | | availability_data | Availability request data template name from template file. |

kw.name: Edit Weekly Data In Availability Request On My Availability Page On Web
kw.doc: Edit ESS My Availability Request on My Availability page. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | effective_date | The week start date for the availability request in offset date format (e.g. 1_1). | | availability_data | Availability request data template name from template file. | | week_start_applicable | Boolean flag indicating if the week start date field is applicable for the availability request. |

kw.name: Delete Availability Request On My Availability Page On Web
kw.doc: Deletes an availability request on the ESS My Availability page as ESS user. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | effective_date_offset | The week start date for the availability request in offset date format (e.g. 1_1). | | week_start_applicable | Boolean flag indicating if the week start date field is applicable for the availability request. |

kw.name: Run Keywords
Clean Up Temporary Availability Request
Get User
kw.msg: Found USER_CREDENTIALS environment variable, attempting to parse Successfully loaded credentials from environment for users: ['SYSADMIN', 'COGNOS_SYSADMIN', 'SM1_STORE1', 'ESS1_STORE1', 'ESS2_STORE1', 'ESS3_STORE1', 'ESS4_STORE1', 'ESS5_STORE1', 'ESS6_STORE1', 'SM1_STORE2', 'ESS1_STORE2', 'ESS2_STORE2', 'ESS3_STORE2', 'ESS4_STORE2', 'ESS5_STORE2', 'ESS6_STORE2', 'SUDO_USER']
kw.doc: Retrieves user data based on user key from credentials or templates.

kw.name: Calculate Date From Week Day Offset
kw.msg: Using base week start date for calculation: 2026-06-21
kw.doc: Calculates a specific date from a week-day offset format.

kw.name: Select Temporary Availability Request From DB
Select Availability Request From DB
Get Config Value
kw.doc: Retrieves a configuration value by key with optional default fallback.

kw.name: Catenate
kw.msg: ${query} = SELECT * FROM RWS_EMP_AVL_DET WHERE PERM_TEMP_IND='T' AND PERSON_ID IN (SELECT PERSON_ID FROM RWS_EMPLOYEE WHERE system_user_id='ZEMP000002' AND OWNER_ID=111110051) AND DATE(EFF_DATE) = DATE('2026-08-...
kw.arg: AND PERSON_ID IN (SELECT PERSON_ID FROM RWS_EMPLOYEE WHERE system_user_id='${system_user_id}'
AND OWNER_ID=${owner_id}) AND DATE(EFF_DATE) = DATE('${eff_date}') AND OWNER_ID=${owner_id};
kw.doc: Catenates the given items together and returns the resulted string.

kw.name: Execute Query And Return Result In Dictionary JSON
Connect To DB2 Database
Connect To Db2 Database
kw.msg: Loaded DB2 credentials from environment variable DB2_CONNECTION
kw.doc: Connect to DB2 database using configuration from environment variables.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.
Establishes connection to DB2 database using configuration from .env file. Connection details are loaded from DB_CONNECTION variable in .env file.

kw.name: Execute Db2 Query For Api Pattern
kw.msg: Executing query: SELECT * FROM RWS_EMP_AVL_DET WHERE PERM_TEMP_IND='T' AND PERSON_ID IN (SELECT PERSON_ID FROM RWS_EM...
kw.doc: Execute a SQL query and return results as a list of dictionaries.

kw.name: Disconnect DB2 Database
Disconnect From Db2 Database
kw.doc: Disconnect from DB2 database and clean up resources.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.
Closes the connection to DB2 database. Example usage: | Disconnect DB2 Database

Execute Query And Return Result In Dictionary JSON Automatically handles database connection and disconnection. Uses DB2_CONNECTION from .env file for database credentials. | =Arguments= | =Description= | | sql_query | SQL query to execute | | =Returns= | =Description= | | json_string | JSON string representing query results. Example: '[{"column1": "value1", "column2": "value2"}, {"column1": "value3", "column2": "value4"}]' | Example usage: | Execute Query And Return Result In Dictionary JSON SELECT * FROM RWS_EMPLOYEE WHERE EMPLOYEE_ID = '50000001'

Select a request from the database. Args: system_user_id (str): The system user ID of requester example 10000001. eff_date (str): The effective date in YYYY-MM-dd format example 2025-03-23. permanent_temp_ind (str): The permanent or temporary indicator P for permanent T for temporary.
Select a request from the database. Args: system_user_id (str): The system user ID of requester example 10000001. eff_date (str): The effective date in YYYY-MM-dd format example 2025-03-23.

kw.name: Get Length
kw.doc: Returns and logs the length of the given item as an integer.

kw.name: Get From Dictionary
kw.doc: Returns a value from the given ``dictionary`` based on the given ``key``.

kw.name: Generate Auth Token With User Credentials
kw.doc: This keyword generates the authentication token for the API. Args: - user_key: The unique key to identify the user in the encrypted file. - base_url: The base URL for the API (optional, defaults to config value)

kw.name: Send A Post Request
kw.doc: This keyword sets the post request for the API.

kw.name: Send A Delete Request
kw.doc: This keyword sets the delete request for the API.

kw.name: Log
kw.msg: No request found for ZEMP000002 on 2026-08-16 to cleanup
kw.arg: No request found for ${ess_user}[username] on ${eff_date} to cleanup
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

Deletes a availability request from the database. The request is identified by the system user ID and effective date. The request is deleted from the RWS_EMP_AVL_DET table. Args: ess_user_key: The user key from users.csv file. eg. ESS-USER1. sm_user_key: The user key from users.csv file. eg. SM-USER1. eff_offset: The effective date in offset for e.g. 1_0.

kw.name: Close Browser
kw.msg: ConnectionError: Playwright process has been terminated with code 130
kw.doc: Closes the current browser.
kw.status: ConnectionError: Playwright process has been terminated with code 130

kw.doc: Executes all the given keywords in a sequence.
kw.status: ConnectionError: Playwright process has been terminated with code 130

test.doc: Verify whether user is able to add, edit, delete temporary availability request and verify notifications on ESS.
test.status:
Execution terminated by signal
Also teardown failed: ConnectionError: Playwright process has been terminated with code 130

suite.doc: Verify Whether User Is Able To Add Edit Delete Temporary Availability Request And Verify Notifications.

errors.msg: Keyword 'Take Screenshot EMBED' could not be run on failure: Playwright process has been terminated with code 130
Calling method '_end_test' of listener 'Browser' failed: ConnectionError: Playwright process has been terminated with code 130
Calling method '_end_suite' of listener 'Browser' failed: ConnectionError: Playwright process has been terminated with code 130
